journal_entry.description: Refinance bank loan — old note extinguished and replaced with new note
journal_entry.rationale: This is a loan refinancing transaction where the old $100,000 loan liability is being extinguished (decreased via debit to Loan Payable) and simultaneously replaced with a new $100,000 loan liability (increased via credit to Loan Payable). The debit tuple (0,0,0,1,0,0) indicates one liability decrease line, and the credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) indicates one liability increase line. Under IFRS, when substantially different loan terms exist, the old liability is derecognized and the new liability is recognized. If terms are substantially the same, it's treated as a modification. In either case, the accounting treatment here reflects the extinguishment of the old obligation and recognition of the new one. No tax implications for loan refinancing. Total debits ($100,000) = Total credits ($100,000).

output.reason: Refinancing replaces an old loan with a new loan. The debit side records the elimination of the old $100 liability (liability decrease). The new $100 loan will be recorded on the credit side. This is a single debit line decreasing the old liability.

debit_classifier.reason: Refinancing replaces an old loan with a new loan. The debit side records the elimination of the old $100 liability (liability decrease). The new $100 loan will be recorded on the credit side. This is a single debit line decreasing the old liability.

output.reason: Refinancing replaces one loan with another. The old $100 loan must be removed from the books (liability decrease) and the new $100 loan must be recorded (liability increase). On the credit side: (1) removing the old loan = liability decrease, which requires a DEBIT, so this is not on the credit side; (2) recording the new loan = liability increase, which requires a CREDIT. Therefore, credit side has 1 line for liability increase.

credit_classifier.reason: Refinancing replaces one loan with another. The old $100 loan must be removed from the books (liability decrease) and the new $100 loan must be recorded (liability increase). On the credit side: (1) removing the old loan = liability decrease, which requires a DEBIT, so this is not on the credit side; (2) recording the new loan = liability increase, which requires a CREDIT. Therefore, credit side has 1 line for liability increase.
